subjects.name: Discrete Mathematics & Graph Theory

units.title: Mathematical Logic
topics: Propositional calculus: statements, connectives, well-formed formulas, truth tables
Tautologies, equivalence of formulas, duality law, tautological implications, normal forms
Theory of inference for statement calculus; consistency of premises; indirect method of proof
Predicate calculus: predicates, quantifiers, free and bound variables, inference theory

units.title: Set Theory
topics: Sets: operations, principle of inclusion-exclusion
Relations: properties, operations, partition and covering, transitive closure
Equivalence, compatibility and partial ordering, Hasse diagrams
Functions: bijective, composition, inverse, permutation, recursive; lattices and properties

units.title: Combinatorics and Recurrence Relations
topics: Basis of counting; permutations (with repetition, circular, restricted); combinations
Binomial and multinomial coefficients and theorems
Generating functions; function of sequences; partial fractions
Recurrence relations: formulation and solving by substitution, generating functions, characteristic roots

units.title: Graph Theory
topics: Basic concepts, applications, subgraphs
Graph representations: adjacency and incidence matrices
Isomorphic graphs, paths and circuits
Eulerian and Hamiltonian graphs

units.title: Multi Graphs
topics: Multigraphs, bipartite and planar graphs; Euler’s theorem
Graph colouring and covering; chromatic number
Spanning trees; Prim’s and Kruskal’s algorithms
BFS and DFS spanning trees

subjects.name: Digital Logic & Computer Organization

units.title: Data Representation and Digital Logic Circuits-I
topics: Binary numbers; fixed- and floating-point representation; number base conversions; octal and hexadecimal
Complements, signed binary numbers, binary codes
Boolean theorems, logic gates, universal gates, minimization; K-map simplification (up to 4 variables)
Combinational circuits: adders, subtractors, encoders, decoders, multiplexers and de-multiplexers

units.title: Digital Logic Circuits-II and Basic Structure of Computers
topics: Sequential circuits, flip-flops, binary counters, registers, shift registers, ripple counters
Computer types, functional units, basic operational concepts
Bus structures, software, performance, multiprocessors and multicomputers
Computer generations; Von Neumann architecture

units.title: Computer Arithmetic and Processor Organization
topics: Addition and subtraction of signed numbers; design of fast adders
Multiplication of positive and signed operands; fast multiplication; integer division
Processor organization: fundamental concepts, execution of a complete instruction
Multiple-bus organization; hardwired and microprogrammed control

units.title: Memory Organization
topics: Basic concepts; semiconductor RAM memories; read-only memories
Speed, size and cost; cache memories; performance considerations
Virtual memories; memory management requirements
Secondary storage

units.title: Input/Output Organization
topics: Accessing I/O devices; interrupts
Direct memory access; buses
Interface circuits
Standard I/O interfaces

units.title: Introduction, AVL and B-Trees
topics: Introduction to algorithm analysis; space and time complexity; asymptotic notations
AVL trees: creation, insertion, deletion and applications

units.title: Heaps, Graphs and Divide and Conquer
topics: Heap trees (priority queues): min and max heaps, operations and applications
Graphs: terminology, representations, search and traversals
Connected and biconnected components and applications

units.title: Greedy Method and Dynamic Programming
topics: Greedy method: job sequencing with deadlines, knapsack, minimum cost spanning trees, single source shortest paths
Dynamic programming: general method, all pairs shortest paths
Bellman-Ford, optimal binary search trees, 0/1 knapsack
String editing, travelling salesperson problem

units.title: Backtracking and Branch and Bound
topics: Backtracking: general method, 8-queens, sum of subsets, graph colouring, 0/1 knapsack
Branch and bound: general method
0/1 knapsack problem
Travelling salesperson problem

units.title: NP-Hard and NP-Complete Problems
topics: Basic concepts of NP-hard and NP-complete
Clique decision problem (CDP)

Travelling salesperson decision problem (TSP)

topics: Class declaration and modifiers; class members; object declaration

seo.title: Full Stack Development - I - JNTUK R23 CSE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 CSE 2-2 Full Stack Development - I (skill course): HTML, CSS, JavaScript, DOM, forms and validation, and an introduction to Node.js.

source.college_source_note: Sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus -- autonomous colleges may adapt content slightly from the university-wide baseline.

subjects.notes: Skill Enhancement Course delivered as experiments covering: lists/links/images; HTML tables, forms and frames; HTML5 and CSS (types, selectors, colour/background/font/text, box model); JavaScript (I/O, type conversion, conditionals and loops, predefined and user-defined objects, functions and events, form validation); Node.js. Name, 0-1-2-2 credits and topic/experiment list confirmed from the source PDF; no unit-wise theory syllabus given, so units left empty.

subjects.name: Design Thinking & Innovation

units.title: Introduction to Design Thinking
topics: Elements and principles of design; basics (dot, line, shape, form)
Principles of design
Introduction to design thinking; history of design thinking
New materials in industry

units.title: Design Thinking Process
topics: Design thinking process: empathize, analyze, ideate and prototype
Implementing the process in driving inventions; design thinking in social innovations
Tools: persona, customer journey map, brainstorming, product development

units.title: Innovation
topics: Art of innovation; difference between innovation and creativity
Role of creativity and innovation in organizations; creativity to innovation
Teams for innovation
Measuring the impact and value of creativity

units.title: Product Design
topics: Problem formation; introduction to product design
Product strategies, product value, product planning, product specifications
Innovation towards product design
Case studies

units.title: Design Thinking in Business Processes
topics: Design thinking applied in business and strategic innovation
Principles that redefine business; business challenges (growth, predictability, change, competition, standardization)
Design thinking for corporate needs and startups
Defining and testing business models, business cases and prototypes

seo.title: Design Thinking & Innovation - JNTUK R23 CSE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 CSE 2-2 Design Thinking & Innovation (common to all branches): design thinking process, innovation, product design and business processes.

source.college_source_note: Sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus -- autonomous colleges may adapt content slightly from the university-wide baseline.

subjects.notes: Common to all branches of engineering. Name, 1-0-2-2 credits and full 5-unit syllabus confirmed by reading the source PDF.

subjects.name: Community Service Project Internship

seo.title: Community Service Project Internship - CSE 2-2
seo.meta_description: JNTUK R23 CSE 2-2 mandatory Community Service Project Internship (8 weeks) during summer vacation.

source.college_source_note: Sourced from an autonomous JNTUK-affiliated college's published syllabus -- autonomous colleges may adapt content slightly from the university-wide baseline.

subjects.notes: NEEDS VERIFICATION: the 2-2 course structure lists a ‘Mandatory Community Service Project Internship of 08 weeks duration during summer vacation’, but the source document gives no credits, evaluation scheme or detailed guidelines. Confirm credits and requirements against official JNTUK internship guidelines before flipping to verified.
